{"step_by_step_thinking": "The documents discuss the purpose of pulpotomy in young permanent teeth. Document [6] mentions that pulpotomy removes the entire coronal pulp while maintaining the health of the remaining radicular portion, increasing the chances of eliminating infected or irreversibly inflamed tissue. Document [26] states that partial pulpotomy is indicated when the duration of pulp exposure has been extensive, or the diameter of exposure exceeds 1.5 mm, and it allows the tooth to remain vital for an extended time. This indicates that the purpose of pulpotomy is to minimize infection and preserve the vitality of the tooth.", "answer_choice": "D"}
